caption:
Bun vs Node.js — the runtime war is real.

Bun is faster at everything: installs, starts, serves, tests. But Node has the ecosystem. Which do you pick?

Which runtime are you shipping with? A or B?

#bun #nodejs #javascript #webdev #coding #programming #developer #runtime #performance #codingtips

a.label: Node.js

b.label: Bun

slides.title: Both win. Different strengths.

verdicts.title: Mature ecosystem. Battle-tested.
verdicts.text: 20 years of packages. Every library works. Every cloud supports it. Production-proven at every scale.

verdicts.title: 10-25x faster. Native TypeScript.
verdicts.text: Built-in bundler, test runner, package manager. No config files. Runs .ts directly. But younger ecosystem.

slides.takeaway: New project? Try Bun. Legacy codebase? Node is fine. Both run the same code.

slides.title: The real benchmarks.
slides.sub: Not synthetic — actual dev workflow speed.

items.text: bun install — 20x faster than npm install
bun app.ts — native TypeScript, no config
bun test — built-in test runner, Jest-compatible
Node: mature ecosystem, every library works

slides.teaser: Next: Import maps — ship JavaScript without a bundler
